completeness.notes: All four required OpenSpec artifacts are present: proposal.md, design.md, tasks.md, and specs/web-app-hub/spec.md. The proposal covers summary, motivation, scope, and success criteria. The design covers NiceGUI application shape, contract preservation, runtime/deployment compatibility, information architecture, Laws of UX, visual direction, read-only safety, and verification strategy. Tasks enumerate 11 numbered checkbox items covering implementation, contract preservation, kind manifests, and no-spend testing.

validation_readiness.notes: All scenarios follow well-formed GIVEN/WHEN/THEN BDD structure. Scenarios cover healthy, degraded, unavailable, stale, reconnect, fallback-polling, and read-only safety states. Task 1.10 specifies no-spend tests for rendering, contract compatibility, degraded source handling, responsive layout, and Laws of UX constraints. Task 1.11 adds the repo validation and smoke check gate. The verification strategy section in design.md is explicit about what no-spend checks must cover.
minor_gaps: No scenario for empty state (no rounds exist at all) — left to implementor judgment.
Priority ordering for 'highest-priority affected source or round' is not defined algorithmically; implementors must apply their own heuristic.

operator_ux_coverage.notes: Six view areas are specified (Overview, Rounds, Round Detail, Inbox, Runtime, Troubleshooting). Progressive disclosure ('one level down') is the governing principle and is operationalized through concrete scenarios. Seven Laws of UX are explicitly named (Hick's, Miller's, Fitts's, Jakob's, Proximity/Common Region, Tesler's, Doherty Threshold) with stated design implications. Responsive layout covers desktop-dense scanning and narrow-viewport collapse. Accessibility basics (keyboard navigation, visible focus, non-color-only status, contrast) are present. Read-only safety is exhaustively specified across load, update, reconnect, fallback, and diagnostic paths.
minor_gaps: Timestamp display (time zones, relative vs absolute) is not specified.
No explicit dark/light mode or theming requirement, which is acceptable for an internal operator console.

protocol_compliance.notes: Change artifacts are confined exclusively to openspec/changes/use-nicegui/ (four files, no code or manifest changes). The delta spec uses the correct # Delta: Web App Hub heading format consistent with existing delta specs. Checkbox task format is correct in tasks.md. At least one delta spec includes both ## MODIFIED Requirements and ## ADDED Requirements sections. Requirement and Scenario headings use the mandated ### Requirement: <name> and #### Scenario: <name> patterns. The MODIFIED section correctly marks Operator Overview (introduced in build-web-app-hub) as being enhanced, and Source Of Truth Preservation and Read Only Initial Interface as being tightened for NiceGUI context.

summary: The use-nicegui OpenSpec artifacts are complete, internally consistent, and protocol-compliant. The spec provides sufficient detail for implementation: contract preservation boundaries are explicit, deployment shape is preserved with documented env var semantics, Laws of UX are named and applied rather than decorative, and the progressive-disclosure architecture is specified through verifiable scenarios. Minor gaps (empty state, priority heuristic, timestamp format) are design-detail questions appropriately left to implementors and do not block implementation. The change is accepted for implementation.
